avalancheActivity.comment: The avalanche risk is considerable above the tree line. The significant wind slab problem must be taken into account. Slab avalanches can be triggered by even a small additional load. There are danger spots in steep terrain adjacent to ridgelines, but also distant from ridgelines, in filled-in bowls and gullies and at terrain transitions. Medium to rarely large avalanches are possible. Spontaneous loose snow avalanches are to be expected, especially from sunny, extreme terrain. Below the tree line, individual gliding avalanches or small, wet loose snow avalanches cannot be ruled out.

snowpackStructure.comment: During the last snowfall event, there was 40 to 80 cm of new fallen snow, depending on altitude and region. Due to strong to stormy winds, extensive prone to triggering snowdrift accumulations have formed, which can contain weak layers and bond poorly with the old snowpack. Inside, the persistent weak layer is usually compact, but often moist up to high altitudes. The snow base, fundament is high up and partly weakened on shady slopes. In the middle and low altitudes, the last snow often lies on bare ground.

tendency.highlights: The wind slab problem will continue on Sunday.

weatherForecast.comment:
On Saturday, the sun will shine frequently on the Salzkammergut mountains. In the Pyhrn-Priel region, on the other hand, there will be more clouds, which could affect the sunshine and visibility more often. The north-westerly wind will usually be moderately strong to brisk. Up to -3 degrees at 1500 metres and -5 degrees at 2000 metres.
On Sunday, dense clouds will persist throughout the day, bringing a few snow showers with snowfall levels around 400 to 700 metres above sea level. Brisk winds from north-west to west at times. Temperatures will remain below average for the end of March. At 1500 m -4 degrees, at 2000 m -6 degrees.

ALBINA.generalHeadline: Significant avalanche danger - slab avalanches can easily be triggered!
